displayName: Australia – Work & Holiday Visa (1 year Work & Travel)
shortDescription: Home Affairs · Subclass 417/462 · 12-month work & travel · Eligible passport required

policyAlert.summary: Confirm eligible passport list, age cap, and current WHM visa fee on homeaffairs.gov.au before quoting. India is not on the standard WHM list — verify country eligibility first.

alert.title: Passport country eligibility
alert.body: Working Holiday visas are only for specific passport countries. Confirm subclass 417 vs 462 and that the client has not held a WHM visa before.

about.label: Description
about.value: Temporary visa for young adults from eligible countries to holiday in Australia and work short-term to fund their trip. Subclass 417 (Working Holiday) or 462 (Work and Holiday) depending on passport country.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Eligible passport · Age 18–30 (35 for some countries) · First WHM visa · Sufficient funds · Health & character · No dependent children accompanying

about.label: Work conditions
about.value: Generally work for any employer up to 6 months; specified work in regional areas may support second WHM — verify current rules.

about.label: After approval
about.value: 12-month stay from first entry; maintain insurance and visa conditions; TFN for lawful work.

redFlagsBanner: If passport country is not eligible or client held WHM before, do not lodge — explore other visa options.

redFlags.title: Ineligible passport country
redFlags.fix: Verify list; do not lodge WHM

redFlags.title: Prior WHM visa held
redFlags.fix: Check visa history; second WHM only if eligible

redFlags.title: Insufficient funds
redFlags.fix: Bank statements, sponsor letter if allowed

redFlags.title: No health insurance
redFlags.fix: OVHC or acceptable policy for full stay

redFlags.title: Employment-focused motive
redFlags.description: File looks like work migration not holiday.
redFlags.fix: Holiday plan, return ties, realistic itinerary

faqs.q: Can Indian passport holders get WHM?
faqs.a: India is not on the standard Working Holiday eligible passport list. Always verify the current Home Affairs list for the client passport country before quoting.
Subclass 417 is Working Holiday for most eligible countries; 462 Work and Holiday applies to specific passport countries with separate caps and rules.
Generally 12 months from first entry. Extensions or second WHM only in limited circumstances — verify current policy.
Typically around AUD 5,000 plus return travel costs. Must be credible and available to the applicant.
WHM allows work to supplement holiday but primary purpose is holiday. Condition 8547 limits work with same employer to 6 months unless exempt.
Generally up to 4 months study permitted — verify current conditions.
Dependants cannot be included on WHM application.
May be available if specified regional work completed on first WHM — verify current requirements.
Often 2–8 weeks but varies by office and season — check global processing times.
Verify current fee on visa pricing estimator — approximately AUD 635 but subject to change.
Yes — adequate health insurance for entire stay is required.
Only if requested by Home Affairs based on profile and countries lived.
Often yes for offshore applicants — follow ImmiAccount instructions.
Generally WHM is applied offshore — verify if client holds bridging visa.
Review refusal letter; may reapply only if circumstances change materially.

faqs.q: What is Description for this service?
faqs.a: Temporary visa for young adults from eligible countries to holiday in Australia and work short-term to fund their trip. Subclass 417 (Working Holiday) or 462 (Work and Holiday) depending on passport country.

faqs.q: What is Eligible applicants for this service?
faqs.a: Eligible passport · Age 18–30 (35 for some countries) · First WHM visa · Sufficient funds · Health & character · No dependent children accompanying

faqs.q: What is Work conditions for this service?
faqs.a: Generally work for any employer up to 6 months; specified work in regional areas may support second WHM — verify current rules.

faqs.q: What is Key authority for this service?
faqs.a: Department of Home Affairs (Australia)

faqs.q: What is After approval for this service?
faqs.a: 12-month stay from first entry; maintain insurance and visa conditions; TFN for lawful work.
2–8w — Country-dependent
AUD ~$635 — Verify on homeaffairs.gov.au

faqs.q: What is the Our approval rate?
faqs.a: 88% — ~76% industry benchmark
12 — + health / police if requested
See fee tab — + govt & insurance
Verify list; do not lodge WHM

faqs.q: What if Age over limit at lodgement?
faqs.a: Confirm age on application date

faqs.q: What if Prior WHM visa held?
faqs.a: Check visa history; second WHM only if eligible

faqs.q: Where to verify WHM eligibility and fees?
faqs.a: immi.homeaffairs.gov.au — eligible passport list, subclass 417/462 pages, and visa pricing estimator.

faqs.q: Where to verify WHM eligibility and fees?
faqs.a: immi.homeaffairs.gov.au — eligible passport list, subclass 417/462 pages, and visa pricing estimator.

compliance: Client service agreement on file before submission
Do not advise working in Australia on visitor visa
proTips: Screen passport country before marketing WHM to client
Confirm age on application date not intake date
Use realistic holiday narrative — not full-time job offer focus
Brief client on 6-month employer rule after grant
postApproval: Send grant letter and entry instructions
Guide TFN, bank account, and super basics

timeline.title: Eligibility screen — passport, age, WHM history

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Initial Work & Holiday counselor content and branded checklist.

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Always verify passport eligibility before fee collection. WHM is not available to all nationalities.

resources.title: Home Affairs — Work and Holiday visa (462)

quiz.question: Primary purpose of WHM visa?
quiz.explanation: WHM is a temporary holiday visa with limited work rights.

quiz.question: Typical maximum initial stay on first WHM?
quiz.explanation: First WHM is generally 12 months from first entry.

quiz.question: Can dependent children be included on WHM?
quiz.explanation: WHM cannot include dependants.

quiz.question: WHM is available to which passport holders?
quiz.explanation: Must verify Home Affairs eligible country list.

quiz.question: Application is generally lodged via:
quiz.explanation: WHM applications are online through ImmiAccount.

quiz.question: Funds generally expected at application?
quiz.explanation: Applicants must show sufficient funds for initial stay.

quiz.question: Condition 8547 generally limits:
options: Work with same employer to 6 months
quiz.explanation: Generally cannot work for same employer more than 6 months.

quiz.question: Health insurance for WHM is:
quiz.explanation: Adequate insurance required for entire stay.

quiz.question: Subclass 417 applies to:
quiz.explanation: 417 is Working Holiday; 462 is Work and Holiday for specific countries.

quiz.question: Prior WHM visa history matters because:
quiz.explanation: Generally only one WHM unless second WHM criteria met.

quiz.question: Second WHM may require:
options: Specified regional work on first WHM
quiz.explanation: Verify current second WHM rules on Home Affairs.

quiz.question: Ineligible passport country should:
options: Be screened out before fee collection
Use visitor visa then convert inside
quiz.explanation: Do not lodge if passport not eligible.

quiz.question: WHM refused — best next step?
options: Review refusal and assess material change
quiz.explanation: Refusal letter guides whether reapplication is viable.

quiz.question: Employment-focused WHM file risk:
options: Officer may doubt genuine holiday intent
quiz.explanation: Holiday must remain primary purpose.

quiz.question: Age for WHM is assessed at:
quiz.explanation: Must meet age cap when applying.

quiz.question: Can Indian passport holders get standard WHM?
quiz.explanation: India is not on standard WHM list — always verify.

quiz.question: Study on WHM is generally limited to:
quiz.explanation: Verify current visa conditions.

quiz.question: Biometrics for WHM:
quiz.explanation: Follow ImmiAccount/VFS instructions.

quiz.question: Return travel evidence shows:
options: Client can depart Australia when required
quiz.explanation: Officer must be satisfied applicant can support stay and depart.

quiz.question: TFN guidance is relevant:
quiz.explanation: Post-grant compliance topic for working holidaymakers.

quiz.question: OVHC stands for:
quiz.explanation: Common acceptable insurance type for WHM.

quiz.question: WHM consultancy red flag:
quiz.explanation: Always screen eligibility before marketing WHM.

quiz.question: WHM visa subclass numbers include:
quiz.explanation: 417 Working Holiday and 462 Work and Holiday.

quiz.question: Genuine temporary entrant for WHM means:
options: Intent to holiday with incidental work
quiz.explanation: Temporary holiday purpose with work to fund travel.

quiz.question: Counselors must never:
quiz.explanation: No approval guarantees.
